description_long: This object is a carved wooden mask featuring a prominent human face with exaggerated features, including large, deep-set eyes and a pronounced nose. Above the main face, a smaller human figure is depicted, gripping the top edge of the mask, adding a sculptural, layered effect. The mask is painted in muted tones of pink, blue, and beige, with subtle shading to accentuate the carved details. The back of the object reveals unfinished wood and a wire loop, likely for hanging or mounting. The overall form is rounded, with the nose and chin projecting outward, giving the mask a three-dimensional presence. The craftsmanship suggests it is a decorative or ceremonial piece, possibly inspired by indigenous or folk art traditions.
description: A carved and painted wooden mask with a large human face and a smaller figure on top, featuring bold facial features and muted colors.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full face, large eyes, pronounced nose, and the smaller figure gripping the top.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the depth of the nose and chin, and the profile of the smaller figure.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the unfinished wood, hollowed interior, and a wire loop for hanging.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the profile of the mask and the layered construction of the figures.
